kits.title: Research Source Scout
kits.summary: A research agent buys ranked market, academic, and news sources, then stops when its mandate cannot fund a fourth source.
kits.businessLogic: Rank sources by relevance, purchase the three planned sources sequentially, retain per-source provenance and evidence, and record the rejected fourth source without creating a paid delivery.

negativePath.outcome: The fourth purchase is rejected on-chain because its exact amount exceeds the mandate's remaining authority.

kits.fixtures: Four stable JSON sources with fixed identifiers, prices, relevance scores, and per-source provenance.

kits.title: Page Snapshot Meter
kits.summary: An agent buys a normalized page snapshot containing metadata, links, word count, and a content hash.
kits.businessLogic: Reject fixture HTML containing script, style, or iframe elements, extract text metadata, and hash the normalized result; this is not a general HTML sanitizer.

negativePath.outcome: Payment for one page cannot unlock a different page because the proof is bound to the exact requested resource.

kits.fixtures: Two bundled HTML fixture pages and one deterministic normalization check covering title, links, and word count; the live result computes a content hash, and the kit makes no browser-rendering claim.

kits.title: Existing API Tollgate
kits.summary: Demonstrate an allowlisted fixture-gateway pattern for a read-only API boundary.
kits.businessLogic: Resolve only allowlisted bundled inventory and weather fixtures and return provenance with no forwarded headers; add real header filtering and timeouts before connecting a live backend.

negativePath.outcome: An upstream route outside the explicit allowlist is rejected before a payment challenge is issued.

kits.fixtures: Two bundled inventory and weather route fixtures with stable payloads and explicit route mappings; no live upstream is called.

kits.title: Paid Tool Gateway
kits.summary: Publish free schema-described tool discovery while charging for selected tool results through a bounded HTTP integration.
kits.businessLogic: Publish free discovery metadata for two fixture tools, require a known fixture identifier, enforce the price ceiling in an offline consumer-policy check, and return result provenance from the fixed live purchase.

negativePath.outcome: The offline consumer-policy check rejects a quoted tool price above its declared ceiling before any payment attempt.

kits.fixtures: Two deterministic supply-risk and document-classification tools with declared fixtureId schemas and fixed outputs.

kits.title: Coding Agent Purchase Hook
kits.summary: The shared live consumer verifies the fixed price, settles once, retries the paid request once, and verifies the returned patch hash; an offline hook vector rejects a repeated 402 in the settled state.
kits.businessLogic: Serve a patch only for the allowlisted fixture host, verify its hash after delivery, and return atomic-write metadata without writing files; the repeated-402 hook check is an offline state-transition vector.

negativePath.outcome: The offline hook transition rejects a repeated 402 in the settled state instead of initiating another payment.

kits.fixtures: Two issue identifiers with deterministic patch artifacts and computed SHA-256 values.

kits.title: Discoverable Service Bazaar
kits.summary: Offline vectors rank bundled service-listing fixtures; the live plan buys the fixed alpha merchant route.
kits.businessLogic: Offline vectors rank listings by schema, price, quality, and latency; the live route accepts only the fixed merchant label and known fixture.

negativePath.outcome: A wrong-merchant listing is excluded even though its declared price, quality, and latency signals would otherwise rank first.

kits.fixtures: Three unsigned catalog fixtures with stable schemas, prices, quality signals, merchant labels, and outputs.

kits.title: Agent Reputation Snapshot
kits.summary: Buy a time-bounded reputation snapshot derived from signed execution events without treating a score as payment authority.
kits.businessLogic: Verify an issuer allowlist, exclude stale events, weight completed and failed outcomes, and return evidence references beside the score.

negativePath.outcome: Events signed by an issuer outside the configured trust set are excluded from the reputation calculation.

kits.fixtures: Four deterministic signed events with fixed ageSeconds values produced by clearly labeled trusted and untrusted local Ed25519 fixture signers.

kits.title: Multi-Agent Workflow Router
kits.summary: A planner purchases research, verification, and synthesis artifacts as an explicit dependency graph.
kits.businessLogic: Offline vectors validate DAG dependencies, stage order, and failure blocking; the live plan purchases the clean-case stages in fixed order.

negativePath.outcome: A failed verification stage prevents synthesis from running.

kits.fixtures: Two deterministic case graphs with staged artifact identifiers and one intentional verification failure.

kits.title: Verifiable Compute Broker
kits.summary: Buy a bounded cryptographic workload and verify the returned digest independently on the consumer side.
kits.businessLogic: Apply fixed work tiers and iteration ceilings, execute a deterministic hash chain, and require the consumer to recompute the expected digest.

negativePath.outcome: The deterministic hash function rejects an iteration count above 512 before hashing begins.

kits.fixtures: Two fixed seeds, two work tiers, one checked deterministic digest prefix, and a maximum iteration count.

kits.title: Private Test Runner
kits.summary: A coding agent buys a deterministic hidden-test-result fixture for a known patch identifier.
kits.businessLogic: Select one of two allowlisted patch identifiers, return its precomputed outcome fixture, omit private test sources, and hash the final pass-or-fail report; this starter does not execute user code.

negativePath.outcome: A patch identifier containing traversal syntax is rejected before fixture lookup.

kits.fixtures: Two precomputed patch-outcome fixtures with stable report hashes and no private test source.

kits.title: Build Notary
kits.summary: Buy a signed fixture statement binding an artifact hash to declared dependency and build-command metadata.
kits.businessLogic: Verify a fixture artifact SHA-256, canonicalize its declared dependency and build-command metadata, sign the statement, and verify that signature offline.

negativePath.outcome: A statement carrying an artifact hash different from the requested artifact fails local verification.

kits.fixtures: Two small artifact-content fixtures with declared dependency arrays, build-command strings, and a clearly identified local fixture signer.

kits.title: Model Route Bazaar
kits.summary: Offline vectors compare fixture quotes and select the lowest-cost eligible provider; the live plan buys that fixed selected provider result.
kits.businessLogic: Offline checks enforce quote freshness plus merchant, quality, and latency eligibility, then choose the lowest price; the live plan serves provenance-marked output from that fixed provider.

negativePath.outcome: A provider quote older than the configured freshness window is rejected before provider selection or payment.

kits.fixtures: Clearly labeled fixture providers with stable quotes, scores, and precomputed outputs; no live-model claim.

kits.title: Rights Receipt
kits.summary: Purchase an exact asset-license version and receive content plus a receipt containing content and terms hashes and the settlement transaction hash.
kits.businessLogic: Select text or data fixtures by asset ID and license version, hash the content and terms, record the settlement transaction hash, and verify the asset, version, content, and terms.

negativePath.outcome: Payment for another asset or license version cannot unlock the requested content.

kits.fixtures: Bundled text and CSV data assets with stable hashes and clearly labeled sample license terms.

kits.title: Data Owner Gateway
kits.summary: The live fixture merchant serves one owner-alpha dataset endpoint and receives payment when the agent unlocks that dataset.
kits.businessLogic: Resolve an owner-scoped dataset fixture, return its observed-at and permitted-use metadata, and verify the content hash without claiming revenue splits.

negativePath.outcome: A dataset mapped to another owner is rejected by the endpoint's owner mapping before a payment challenge.

kits.fixtures: Two owner-specific dataset fixtures with stable hashes, observed-at timestamps, and permitted-use metadata.

kits.title: Human Review Outbox
kits.summary: An agent buys a signed review decision only after a case reaches a ready state and the mandate remains valid.
kits.businessLogic: Serve two ready-state fixture decisions, validate a fixture HMAC signature, and demonstrate contract-mandate expiry before settlement.

negativePath.outcome: A distinct short-lived mandate expires before settlement against the ready delayed-case fixture, and the contract rejects payment.

kits.fixtures: Two ready-state deterministic decisions signed by a clearly labeled fixture reviewer; one uses a queue duration beyond the example SLA.

kits.title: Cold-Chain Passport
kits.summary: Buy a shipment passport summarizing custody handoffs, temperature excursions, and sensor continuity.
kits.businessLogic: Calculate threshold duration, detect telemetry gaps, order custody transfers, and bind the passport to one hash covering its telemetry and custody fixtures.

negativePath.outcome: A missing sensor interval produces an explicit incomplete passport rather than an unsupported compliance pass.

kits.fixtures: Deterministic telemetry and custody records for normal, breached, and incomplete shipments.

kits.title: Carbon-Aware Run Window
kits.summary: Buy the lowest-emission execution window that still meets a workload deadline and capacity requirement.
kits.businessLogic: Search bounded time windows, score carbon and capacity, enforce a deadline, and reject forecasts that are too old for scheduling.

negativePath.outcome: A forecast older than the scheduling freshness limit is rejected before a run window is selected.

kits.fixtures: Fixed hourly intensity, capacity, and workload records with one independently checked optimal window.

kits.title: Fleet Corridor Authority
kits.summary: A fleet agent buys two planned route clearances; then the mandate user revokes authority and the third planned settlement fails closed.
kits.businessLogic: Offline vectors validate segment order and weather epoch; the live plan purchases segments 1 and 2 before revocation blocks segment 3.

negativePath.outcome: After two accepted segments the user revokes authority and the third settlement is rejected by the contract.

kits.fixtures: Three deterministic route segments with fixed order, endpoints, clearance decisions, and one route-level weather epoch.

kits.title: Payment Receipt Firewall
kits.summary: A reference merchant demonstrates persistent replay admission, exact request binding, and safe recovery after restart.
kits.businessLogic: Persist used-proof claims, compare origin, method, and resource, record exact response bytes before delivery, and recover the same claim after restart.

negativePath.outcome: After restart the exact same proof returns the byte-identical stored response without repeating verification or paid fulfillment.

kits.fixtures: Two stable vault resources; the live demo purchases one and reuses its exact Stellar testnet proof after restart.

kits.title: Procurement Guard
kits.summary: A purchasing agent evaluates approved vendor quote fixtures offline and buys one explicitly selected allowlisted quote pack.
kits.businessLogic: Offline vectors rank approved fixture quotes by cost, lead time, and freshness; the live route serves one explicitly selected allowlisted quote pack.

negativePath.outcome: A cheaper vendor outside the configured allowlist remains ineligible and cannot be selected for purchase.

kits.fixtures: Three stable quote fixtures with vendor IDs, lead times, quote ages, item rows, and one checked approved-vendor selection.
